steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the corresponding angles property with parallel lines
steps.content: BD is parallel to AE, so line CE is a transversal cutting both parallel lines. Angles AEC and BDC are corresponding angles in this configuration. By the corresponding angles property: $$\angle AEC = \angle BDC = 58^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Find angle CAE using the triangle angle sum
steps.content: In triangle ACE, the angles sum to 180°. We know $\angle AEC = 58^{\circ}$ and $\angle ACE = 62^{\circ}$, so: $$\angle CAE + 62^{\circ} + 58^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$ $$\angle CAE = 60^{\circ}$$ That matches choice B — ‘60°’ is correct.
